# Left PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 229.248 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.6990
  biquad b0=1.00152694112 b1=-1.99237303274 b2=0.991070381746 a1=-1.99237303274 a2=0.99259732287
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 440.186 Hz Gain -4.340 dB Q 4.1252
  biquad b0=0.998244890784 b1=-1.99024864227 b2=0.99283001104 a1=-1.99024864227 a2=0.991074901824
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 1714.234 Hz Gain +2.770 dB Q 3.4961
  biquad b0=1.00505817106 b1=-1.96065730092 b2=0.968004579362 a1=-1.96065730092 a2=0.973062750426
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 109.131 Hz Gain +2.590 dB Q 4.4531
  biquad b0=1.00023987054 b1=-1.99856820995 b2=0.998379320711 a1=-1.99856820995 a2=0.998619191254
Filter 5: ON PK Fc 317.139 Hz Gain +0.864 dB Q 4.8195
  biquad b0=1.00021378651 b1=-1.9954808649 b2=0.995697021795 a1=-1.9954808649 a2=0.99591080831
